paper_title: Pollen Season Analysis and Study Site Description

methods.description: This method calculates the start and end of the pollen season using the 95% and 90% methods, respectively. It also calculates various season parameters such as season duration, annual total, SPI value, maximum pollen concentration, and the day of maximum concentration. Descriptive statistics are used to characterize the season.

steps.description: Pollen seasons were calculated using the 95% and 90% methods for the start and end of the season, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pollen season start and end using 95% and 90% methods

steps.description: The start of the season was defined as the date when 2.5% of the seasonal cumulative pollen count was trapped and the end of the season as the date when 95% of the seasonal cumulative pollen count was reached.
steps.searchable_summary: define start and end of pollen season based on cumulative pollen count

steps.description: Apart from the season start and end date, the following season parameters were calculated: season duration, annual total, SPI value, the maximum pollen concentration in the season and the day of maximum concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate season parameters including duration, total, SPI, max concentration

steps.description: Descriptive statistics was used to calculate the season characteristics (minimum, maximum, quartile 1 and 3; arithmetic mean, confidence interval for arithmetic mean, standard deviation, median and coefficient of variation).
steps.searchable_summary: perform descriptive statistics on season characteristics

methods.description: This method analyzes the relationship between pollen season parameters and meteorological data using Spearman's rank correlation test. Meteorological parameters include air temperature, rainfall, relative humidity, cloudiness, and relative sunshine.

steps.description: To find the relationship between season and meteorological parameters, the following meteorological parameters were used: Air temperature, Rainfall, Relative humidity, Cloudiness, Relative sunshine.
Spearman's rank correlation test was used to find the relationship between season and meteorological parameters.
